// FROM THE STUDIO / EST. 2019

WE DON'T
RESTOCK.
EVER.

01 — LIMITED RUN

CUT ONCE,
NUMBERED.

Every drop is sized to the dead-stock we find. Numbered, hand-tagged, capped. No restocks, no reissues, no sneaky re-runs in a slightly different colorway. When it's gone, it's archive.

02 — DEAD-STOCK FIRST

WHAT WE FIND,
WHAT WE MAKE.

We work backwards from the fabric — dead-stock, deadstock, and recycled mill ends sourced from Tokyo, Como and Lancashire. Then we cut the pattern. Then we make the drop.

03 — MADE TO MOVE

TESTED ON
WORKING DAYS.

Every garment is built and broken on actual working days — couriers, builders, bartenders, skaters. If a seam blows out, we change the seam. The wearer is the spec sheet.

FRAME 01 / 08 · ON BODY · NIKO
● LOW · 14 LEFT
// DETAIL / CONSTRUCTION

BUILT FOR
WET CHAIN SHIFTS.

01 — FABRIC

Dead-stock 2.4oz Japanese ripstop nylon, originally cut for a workwear brand that closed in 2019. Densely woven, water-resistant, breaks in for years.

02 — SEAMS

All major seams are taped and double-stitched. Stress points (shoulder, seat, cuff) get a triple bar-tack and a back-tack. Field-replaceable.

03 — HARDWARE

YKK Excella main zip in matte gunmetal. ITW Nexus side-release buckles. Solid brass shanks on every button. Nothing plastic on the outside.

04 — POCKETS

Five total — two side cargo, two chest, one internal phone. Cargo pockets sized for an A5 notebook and a U-lock respectively. We checked.

NIKO M.
2 WEEKS AGO · LONDON E2
★★★★★

Six weeks of cycle-courier shifts. Three rain days. Still bone dry. The cargo pockets fit the dispatch tablet and a full water bottle on either side — Pulse actually thought about what we carry.

RIN T.
1 MONTH AGO · BERLIN F'HAIN
★★★★★

I size down on Pulse usually — sized to body on this one. Still oversized but doesn't drown me. The hood actually fits over a beanie which is a first for any coat I own. Magma colorway is louder in person, in a good way.

ASH P.
2 MONTHS AGO · TOKYO N3
★★★★☆

Knocked one off because the zip pull is a touch small for gloved hands. Otherwise: the best ripstop coat I've owned, and I've owned many. Built like it expects to be passed down. I asked for a longer zip pull — they sent one. Wild service.

// PRODUCTION NOTES

SHOT IN
FOUR DAYS.
NO RESHOOTS.

Drop 07 was shot across four Wednesdays in February, between 4:30 and 6:50 pm. The light was decided in advance — we just walked. Wearers were friends, couriers, the studio's neighbour upstairs.

We didn't reshoot anything. If a frame missed, it missed. The film roll order is the lookbook order. Rosa drew the contact sheet on the studio wall and we picked twenty-two with a marker.

LIVE NOW — DROP 07 / NEUTRAL HEAT FREE SHIPPING / ORDERS > $120 STUDIO OPEN WED – SAT
PULSE//WORKS
Drop 07 Shop Lookbook Archive Studio Stores
SEARCH EN / USD CART (2)
// THE STUDIO / EST. 2019

SEVEN DROPS
SINCE 2019.

Pulse//Works is a six-person studio in Hackney making numbered workwear from dead-stock fabric. We don't restock, we don't sell on Amazon, and we don't have a marketing team. This page tells you who we are and why.

UNIT 4 · BOHEMIA PLACE · E8 1DT
SECOND FLOOR
OF AN OLD STABLE BLOCK.
SIX DESKS
ONE CUTTING TABLE
TWO INDUSTRIAL MACHINES
// 01 — HOW WE GOT HERE

STARTED
BECAUSE
NOTHING FIT.

Pulse started in 2019 because the founder, a courier in East London, kept ripping his work pants every six weeks. He bought another pair. Ripped those too. So he asked a friend who'd just finished at LCF to make him something that wouldn't, and paid her in beer.

She did. They lasted seventeen months. He wore them out cycling, lifting, falling, getting back up. By the end of year one, she'd made nineteen more pairs for nineteen friends — all couriers, all paid in beer.

Drop 01 launched in October 2019 from a single sheet of butcher paper taped to a wall. Forty pairs of pants, sold in four hours, never restocked. Drop 02 was a coat. Drop 03 was a vest. We've kept the rule: we don't make a thing twice.

Today the studio is six people across pattern, production, photography and shipping. We make seven drops a year. Every garment is numbered, hand-tagged, sized to the dead-stock fabric we manage to find. When it's gone, it's gone — and that's the point.

// 02 — THE SIX

THE PEOPLE.

SIX DESKS · ONE CUTTING TABLE
SAM K.
FOUNDER · DIRECTION

Ex-courier. Still rides. Makes most of the calls and breaks most of the seams.

YUKI N.
PATTERN · CUT

Trained in Tokyo, lives in Stoke Newington. Made the original pants. Still cuts every coat.

ROSA M.
PHOTOGRAPHY · FILM

Shoots every drop on TRI-X, on the street, no reshoots. Refuses to own a softbox.

BENNI O.
PRODUCTION · QA

Stitches everything that isn't on the long machine. Tests every garment for a week before it ships.

ASH P.
SOURCING · TEXTILES

Travels to Tokyo, Como, Lancashire. Knows the back rooms of every closed mill in the EU.

RIN T.
SHIPPING · REPAIR

Packs every order by hand and runs the field-repair desk. Has personally restitched 412 garments.

// 03 — THE FIVE RULES

FIVE THINGS
WE DON'T DO.

01
WE DON'T RESTOCK.

Capped, numbered, finished. Even if the entire drop sells in twelve minutes, that's the drop.

02
WE DON'T DISCOUNT.

No Black Friday. No end-of-season. No "members only" sale. The price is the price, day one.

03
WE DON'T USE NEW.

86% of our fabric is dead-stock. We use what mills couldn't, didn't, or stopped wanting to.

04
WE DON'T AB-TEST.

No focus groups, no demographic targeting. We make for ourselves and our friends, and you might be one.

05
WE DON'T BIN IT.

Send any Pulse garment back, any year. We repair it free. We've done 412 so far. We'll do yours.

// 04 — HOW A DROP HAPPENS

12 WEEKS,
START TO SHIP.

W01
SOURCE

Ash flies to a closed mill. Comes back with fabric and a quantity we cannot exceed.

W03
PATTERN

Yuki drafts a pattern that uses every meter. The shape of the fabric decides the shape of the drop.

W05
SAMPLE

Benni cuts a sample. Sam wears it on the bike for two weeks. We revise until it survives.

W08
PRODUCE

We make 80% in-house. Anything we can't, we send to a workshop in Wood Green we've used for six years.

W11
SHOOT

Rosa shoots for four Wednesdays, available light, no reshoots. Twenty-two frames. One contact sheet.

W12
DROP

Rin packs every order by hand. Newsletter goes 24 hours before public. Then it goes live.

// 05 — IN PRINT

PRESS.

DOWNLOAD PRESS KIT →
DAZED
FEB 2026 · ON DROP 07

"Pulse Works has quietly become the most thoughtful workwear label in the UK — without ever raising its voice."

HIGHSNOBIETY
NOV 2025 · ON DROP 06

"The anti-restock model isn't a gimmick — it's a worldview, and a kind of relief from the rest of the industry."

WALLPAPER*
OCT 2025 · STUDIO VISIT

"Six desks, two industrial machines, and the most polite refusal of fashion's gravitational pull we've seen this decade."

MONOCLE
MAY 2025 · ON SOURCING

"An object lesson in what the dead-stock economy could be, if the rest of the industry agreed to slow down."

// VISIT / WRITE / CALL

SAY HELLO.

We answer every email within 48 hours. We answer the phone Wed – Sat between 12 and 18. The studio is open by appointment — book ahead and we'll put the kettle on.

STUDIO
Unit 4, Bohemia Place
London E8 1DT, UK
